summary: A concise map of the major Bittensor platform components: subnets, the blockchain, TAO, role families, tools, and network environments.

summary: How Bittensor distinguishes network-level blockchain validators from subnet-level validators.

summary: How effective stake describes the total staked TAO associated with a delegate.

summary: How an epoch marks a Bittensor subnet's consensus period.

summary: How exponential moving averages help Bittensor smooth noisy flow, bond, and reward signals over time.

title: Extrinsics

summary: How Bittensor governance uses Triumvirate proposals and Senate approval to review privileged network changes.

summary: How Bittensor uses supply-based TAO and subnet alpha halvings to reduce emission rates across network and subnet tokenomics.

summary: How an immunity period gives a newly registered Bittensor subnet participant temporary protection from deregistration.

summary: How pending transaction visibility creates MEV risk in Bittensor before block inclusion.

title: Subnet 91: Bitstarter #1
summary: Subnet 91 is a Bittensor subnet slot whose live Finney identity is registered as Bitstarter #1, with launch-oriented metadata and no public repository currently listed.

title: Subnet 92: TensorClaw
summary: TensorClaw is Bittensor Subnet 92, a decentralized LLM-inference network that aggregates many model-serving backends into one load-balanced, high-availability API and rewards miners for the real request traffic they handle well.

title: Subnet 93: Bitcast
summary: Bitcast is Bittensor Subnet 93, a creator-economy subnet where miners publish YouTube videos that fulfill brand briefs and validators verify real audience engagement before rewards are paid.

title: Subnet 94: BitSota
summary: BitSota is Bittensor Subnet 94, a decentralized research network where miners automatically evolve machine-learning algorithms to beat a benchmark and validators independently re-evaluate the results.

title: Subnet 95: Unknown
summary: Subnet 95 is a Bittensor subnet slot that currently has no registered Finney identity, so its public project name, repository, and technical mechanism should be treated as unverified.

title: Subnet 96: Verathos
summary: Verathos is Bittensor Subnet 96, a decentralized compute network where miners run AI inference and attach cryptographic proofs that the computation was correct, which validators verify cheaply before rewarding honest work.

title: Subnet 97: Albedo
summary: Albedo is Bittensor Subnet 97, a king-of-the-hill competition where miners submit challenger language models that are judged head-to-head against a reigning champion, with full match transcripts published for research.

title: Subnet 98: ForeverMoney
summary: ForeverMoney is Bittensor Subnet 98, an automated liquidity-management subnet where miners propose pool-rebalancing strategies and validators score them through forward simulations, executing the winners on-chain.

title: Subnet 99: Leoma
summary: Leoma is Bittensor Subnet 99, an AI video subnet where miners run text-image-to-video models and validators score the generated clips, setting winner-take-all weights on-chain each round.
